rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-building section as specific prior field evidence that coupon-induced gains can increase spending beyond a pure budget-constraint (income) effect, including spillovers to other products. The citing authors even summarize Heilman et al.'s proposed mechanisms in a footnote, which indicates functional use rather than a passing mention. They later return to this citation when interpreting why usage rises even beyond the free allowance under three-part tariffs. This is conceptual grounding, not method or data reuse.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Heilman et al. (2002) as behavioral and empirical precedent that free or unexpected promotions can raise consumption beyond direct monetary effects and spill over beyond the focal discounted unit. It uses that precedent to motivate and interpret its core claim that free-minute allowances increase overall service usage beyond what standard budget-shift predictions imply.
